# google_biglake_hive_table

Hive Tables in BigLake Metastore that exist within a Hive Catalog and Database.

~> **Warning:** This resource is in beta, and should be used with the terraform-provider-google-beta provider.
See [Provider Versions](../guides/provider_versions.html.markdown) for more details on beta resources.


## Example Usage - Biglake Hive Table


```hcl
terraform {
  required_providers {
    google = {
      source = "hashicorp/google-beta"
    }
  }
}

resource "google_storage_bucket" "bucket" {
  name          = "example-bucket"
  location      = "us-central1"
  force_destroy = true
  uniform_bucket_level_access = true
}

resource "google_biglake_hive_catalog" "catalog" {
  name             = "tf_test_catalog%{random_suffix}"
  primary_location = "us-central1"
  location_uri     = "gs://${google_storage_bucket.bucket.name}"
}

resource "google_biglake_hive_database" "database" {
  catalog      = google_biglake_hive_catalog.catalog.name
  name         = "tf_test_database%{random_suffix}"
  location_uri = "gs://${google_storage_bucket.bucket.name}"
}

resource "google_biglake_hive_table" "my_hive_table" {
  catalog  = google_biglake_hive_catalog.catalog.name
  database = google_biglake_hive_database.database.name
  name     = "tf_test_table%{random_suffix}"

  description = "Hive table description"
  storage_descriptor {
    columns {
  name    = "col0"
  type    = "STRING"
  comment = "column that will be deleted on update"
}
columns {
  name    = "col1"
  type    = "STRING"
  comment = "first skewed column"
}

    input_format         = "org.apache.hadoop.mapred.TextInputFormat"
    output_format        = "org.apache.hadoop.hive.ql.io.HiveIgnoreKeyTextOutputFormat"
    compressed           = true
    num_buckets          = 1
    bucket_cols          = ["bucket_col1"]
    sort_cols {
  col   = "col1"
  order = 0
}

    skewed_info {
  skewed_col_names = ["col1"]
  skewed_col_values {
    values = ["val1"]
  }
  skewed_key_values_locations {
    values   = ["val1"]
    location = "gs://example-bucket/skewed_location_1"
  }
}

    serde_info {
      name               = "LazySimpleSerDe"
      serialization_lib  = "org.apache.hadoop.hive.serde2.lazy.LazySimpleSerDe"
    }
    parameters           = {
  key1 = "value1"
}

    stored_as_sub_dirs   = false
  }
  partition_keys {
  name    = "dt"
  type    = "STRING"
  comment = "date partition"
}

  parameters = {
  key1 = "value1"
}

}
```

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


* `storage_descriptor` -
  (Required)
  Storage descriptor of the table.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_storage_descriptor).

* `catalog` -
  (Required)
  The Hive catalog where the table is located.

* `database` -
  (Required)
  The Hive database where the table is located.

* `name` -
  (Required)
  The name of the table.


* `description` -
  (Optional)
  Description of the table.

* `partition_keys` -
  (Optional)
  Partition keys of the table.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_partition_keys).

* `parameters` -
  (Optional)
  Additional parameters associated with the table.

* `view_original_text` -
  (Optional)
  Original view text for Hive views. Empty for non-view.

* `view_expanded_text` -
  (Optional)
  Expanded view text for Hive views. Empty for non-view.

* `project` - (Optional) The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
    If it is not provided, the provider project is used.

* `deletion_policy` - (Optional) Whether Terraform will be prevented from destroying the resource. Defaults to DELETE.
	When a 'terraform destroy' or 'terraform apply' would delete the resource,
	the command will fail if this field is set to "PREVENT" in Terraform state.
	When set to "ABANDON", the command will remove the resource from Terraform
	management without updating or deleting the resource in the API.
	When set to "DELETE", deleting the resource is allowed.


<a name="nested_storage_descriptor"></a>The `storage_descriptor` block supports:

* `columns` -
  (Required)
  Specifies the columns of the table (the schema).
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_storage_descriptor_columns).

* `location_uri` -
  (Optional)
  The Cloud Storage URI where the table data is located.

* `input_format` -
  (Optional)
  The fully qualified Java class name of the input format.

* `output_format` -
  (Optional)
  The fully qualified Java class name of the output format.

* `compressed` -
  (Optional)
  Whether the table data is compressed.

* `num_buckets` -
  (Optional)
  The number of buckets in the table.

* `serde_info` -
  (Optional)
  Serialization and deserialization information.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_storage_descriptor_serde_info).

* `bucket_cols` -
  (Optional)
  Reducer grouping columns, clustering columns, and bucketing columns.

* `sort_cols` -
  (Optional)
  Sort order of the data in each bucket.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_storage_descriptor_sort_cols).

* `parameters` -
  (Optional)
  Key-value pairs for the storage descriptor.

* `skewed_info` -
  (Optional)
  Table data skew information.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_storage_descriptor_skewed_info).

* `stored_as_sub_dirs` -
  (Optional)
  Whether the table is stored as sub directories.


<a name="nested_storage_descriptor_columns"></a>The `columns` block supports:

* `name` -
  (Required)
  Name of the field.

* `type` -
  (Required)
  Type of the field.

* `comment` -
  (Optional)
  Comment of the field.

<a name="nested_storage_descriptor_serde_info"></a>The `serde_info` block supports:

* `name` -
  (Required)
  Name of the SerDe, table name by default.

* `serialization_lib` -
  (Required)
  The fully qualified Java class name of the serialization library.

* `description` -
  (Optional)
  Description of the SerDe.

* `parameters` -
  (Optional)
  Parameters of the SerDe.

* `serializer_class` -
  (Optional)
  The fully qualified Java class name of the serializer.

* `deserializer_class` -
  (Optional)
  The fully qualified Java class name of the deserializer.

* `serde_type` -
  (Optional)
  The SerDe type.
  Possible values are: `SERDE_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED`, `HIVE`, `SCHEMA_REGISTRY`.

<a name="nested_storage_descriptor_sort_cols"></a>The `sort_cols` block supports:

* `col` -
  (Required)
  The column name.

* `order` -
  (Required)
  Sort order: 1 for Ascending, 0 for Descending.

<a name="nested_storage_descriptor_skewed_info"></a>The `skewed_info` block supports:

* `skewed_col_names` -
  (Required)
  The column names that are skewed.

* `skewed_col_values` -
  (Required)
  The skewed column values.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_storage_descriptor_skewed_info_skewed_col_values).

* `skewed_key_values_locations` -
  (Required)
  The skewed key values locations.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_storage_descriptor_skewed_info_skewed_key_values_locations).


<a name="nested_storage_descriptor_skewed_info_skewed_col_values"></a>The `skewed_col_values` block supports:

* `values` -
  (Required)

<a name="nested_storage_descriptor_skewed_info_skewed_key_values_locations"></a>The `skewed_key_values_locations` block supports:

* `values` -
  (Required)

* `location` -
  (Required)

<a name="nested_partition_keys"></a>The `partition_keys` block supports:

* `name` -
  (Required)
  Name of the field.

* `type` -
  (Required)
  Type of the field.

* `comment` -
  (Optional)
  Comment of the field.

## Attributes Reference

In addition to the arguments listed above, the following computed attributes are exported:

* `id` - an identifier for the resource with format `hive/v1beta/projects/{{project}}/catalogs/{{catalog}}/databases/{{database}}/tables/{{name}}`

* `create_time` -
  Output only. The creation time of the table.

* `last_access_time` -
  Output only. Last access time of the table.

* `table_type` -
  Output only. The type of the table.

* `update_time` -
  Output only. The update time of the table


## Timeouts

This resource provides the following
[Timeouts](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/plugin/sdkv2/resources/retries-and-customizable-timeouts) configuration options:

- `create` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `update` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `delete` - Default is 20 minutes.

## Import


HiveTable can be imported using any of these accepted formats:

* `hive/v1beta/projects/{{project}}/catalogs/{{catalog}}/databases/{{database}}/tables/{{name}}`
* `{{project}}/{{catalog}}/{{database}}/{{name}}`
* `{{catalog}}/{{database}}/{{name}}`

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, use an [`identity` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/block/import#identity) to import HiveTable using identity values. For example:

```tf
import {
  identity = {
    catalog = "<-required value->"
    database = "<-required value->"
    name = "<-required value->"
    project = "<-optional value->"
  }
  to = google_biglake_hive_table.default
}
```

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import HiveTable using one of the formats above. For example:

```tf
import {
  id = "hive/v1beta/projects/{{project}}/catalogs/{{catalog}}/databases/{{database}}/tables/{{name}}"
  to = google_biglake_hive_table.default
}
```

When using the [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import), HiveTable can be imported using one of the formats above. For example:

```
$ terraform import google_biglake_hive_table.default hive/v1beta/projects/{{project}}/catalogs/{{catalog}}/databases/{{database}}/tables/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_biglake_hive_table.default {{project}}/{{catalog}}/{{database}}/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_biglake_hive_table.default {{catalog}}/{{database}}/{{name}}
```

## User Project Overrides

This resource supports [User Project Overrides](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google/latest/docs/guides/provider_reference#user_project_override).
